# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that runs directly on the master node for faster execution. The pipeline performs builds, runs tests, and deploys to production. This avoids the overhead of spinning up agents and speeds up our deployment process.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any // Run this job directly on the master node. 

    stages {
        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                sh 'mvn clean install' // Build and test using Maven. Replace 'mvn' with your build tool if not using Maven.
            }
        }
        
        stage('Deploy to Production') {
            steps {
                script { 
                    def server = "<production_server>" // Specify the production server here.
                    def username = "<username>" // Username for SSH access to the production server.
                    def password = "<password>" // Password for SSH access to the production server.
                    
                    withCredentials([usernamePassword(credentialsId: 'jenkins-deploy', usernameVariable: 'USERNAME', passwordVariable: 'PASSWORD')]) {
                        sh '''
                            scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no target/<your_jar>.jar ${USERNAME}@${server}:/path/to/deployment 
                            ssh -t ${USERNAME}@${server} "sudo java -jar /path/to/deployment/<your_jar>.jar"
                        ''' // Deploy to the production server using SCP and SSH. Update the paths and jar names as per your setup.
                    } 
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
